title: Glass Refraction Lab
description: The user operates a virtual optics laboratory where they examine three glass objects under different lighting and post-processing conditions. By cycling through refraction modes, adjusting environment intensity, toggling polarization filters via the sunglasses, and shattering/restoring the broken window, the user explores how light interacts with transparent materials.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 (via importmap from cdn.jsdelivr.net) that implements an interactive Glass Refraction Lab. Load three GLB assets using GLTFLoader: GlassVaseFlowers.glb placed center-left, SunglassesKhronos.glb floating center, and GlassBrokenWindow.glb as a large pane center-right. Use an HDR-style environment by setting scene.environment to a PMREMGenerator-processed scene with colored point lights (warm key light upper-left, cool fill lower-right, rim light behind). Set renderer with toneMapping ACESFilmic and outputColorSpace SRGBColorSpace. Apply MeshPhysicalMaterial overrides to all glass-like meshes: transmission 0.95, roughness 0.05, ior 1.5, thickness 0.5, transparent true. Camera starts at (0,1.5,4) looking at origin. Add OrbitControls with damping. Implement these interactions requiring parameter validation: (1) A dropdown select with 5 refraction presets (Diamond ior=2.42, Water ior=1.33, Glass ior=1.5, Crystal ior=2.0, Air ior=1.0) that updates all glass materials and validates ior is within 1.0-2.5. (2) A slider controlling environment intensity from 0 to 5 that updates scene.environmentIntensity, validated to that range. (3) Clicking the sunglasses toggles a polarization post-processing effect implemented as a full-screen shader pass that desaturates and darkens the scene (simulating polarized lens view), toggling a boolean state. (4) A button that animates the broken window pane: on click, child meshes scatter outward with random velocities over 1 second using requestAnimationFrame lerp; clicking again reverses the animation to restore the window, tracking animation state. (5) A roughness slider (0 to 1) that adjusts roughness on all glass materials with validation. (6) A button to cycle through 3 background colors (dark gray, midnight blue, deep purple) updating renderer.setClearColor. Display current parameter values in an overlay div. All parameter changes must be validated before applying. Add subtle rotation animation to the sunglasses model. Include an HTML overlay panel styled with semi-transparent dark background and white text positioned top-left for all controls.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  refractionIor: number,         // current index of refraction applied to all glass materials (1.0-2.5)
  environmentIntensity: number,         // scene environment light intensity multiplier (0-5)
  polarizationActive: boolean,         // whether sunglasses polarization filter is enabled
  windowShattered: boolean,         // whether the broken window is in shattered state
  activePresetIndex: number,         // index of currently selected refraction preset (0-4)
  glassObjectsLoaded: number,         // count of successfully loaded GLB assets (0-3)
  totalMeshesWithGlass: number,         // count of meshes with glass material applied
  cameraDistance: number,         // distance from camera to origin in world units
  renderToneMapping: number,         // tone mapping mode constant from THREE
  lastInteractionTimestamp: number,         // milliseconds since epoch of last user interaction
}
